content:
```json
{
  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "Logic changes to workflow reminder cancellation lack proper synchronization and have potential race conditions with scheduled task deletion.",
  "circularTests": {
    "detected": false,
    "count": 0,
    "total": 0,
    "details": ""
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Race Condition / Data Integrity",
      "file": "packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleEmailReminders.ts",
      "line": 52,
      "title": "Unsafe reminder deletion without job cancellation verification",
      "description": "The code deletes WorkflowReminder records from the database (line 70-73) after attempting to cancel SendGrid scheduled sends, but does not verify that the cancellation succeeded before deletion. If the SendGrid API call fails silently or partially, the database record is still deleted, leaving orphaned scheduled jobs that will execute. Additionally, `Promise.all(workflowRemindersToDelete)` at line 76 executes parallel deletes without transaction protection.",
      "suggestion": "Verify the SendGrid cancellation response before deleting the database record. Use a database transaction to ensure atomicity of the cancellation + deletion operations. Only delete the reminder if the SendGrid API returns success status."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Missing Job Cancellation",
      "file": "packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts",
      "line": null,
      "title": "Scheduled SMS reminders deleted from DB without canceling underlying jobs",
      "description": "The refactored code calls `deleteScheduledSMSReminder(reminder.id, reminder.referenceId)` for SMS reminders but previously this function was only called when `reminder.scheduled` was true. The new code removes this check entirely. Without verifying that the reminder was actually scheduled before attempting deletion, the function may fail silently or leave scheduled SMS jobs in the queue system while the DB record is deleted.",
      "suggestion": "Restore the `reminder.scheduled` check before calling delete functions, or ensure deleteScheduledSMSReminder handles the case where the job doesn't exist in the queue gracefully and logs the result."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Error Handling",
      "file": "packages/features/bookings/lib/handleNewBooking.ts",
      "line": 974,
      "title": "Broad catch-all error handling masks reminder cancellation failures",
      "description": "The try-catch block at line 971-977 catches all errors during workflow reminder cancellation but only logs them. If reminder cancellation fails, the rescheduling proceeds silently, leaving orphaned reminders for the old booking that may still fire. The error is not re-thrown or properly surfaced.",
      "suggestion": "Either propagate critical errors to fail the reschedule operation, or implement retry logic with exponential backoff. At minimum, distinguish between retriable and non-retriable errors."
    }
  ]
}
```
